data.title: China’s rare-earth squeeze proves minerals are weapons the U.S. must arm up
data.description:
China cut off exports of seven rare earth minerals and the high-powered magnets made from them, targeting global industries that rely on these materials to operate. These minerals power missiles, fighter jets, drones, submarines, electric vehicles, data centres, wind turbines, and the entire clean energy transition. By halting their flow, China used minerals like weapons […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/china-minerals-are-weapons-u-s-must-arm-up/

data.title: Trump Advocates for Lower U.S. Interest Rates
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his desire for the United States to reduce interest rates to between 1% and 2%. He emphasized that he does not want to pay higher rates on 10-year debt.

data.title: Binance will adjust the portfolio margin collateral ratio
data.description: According to the official announcement, Binance will adjust the collateral ratio of some assets under the portfolio margin model at 06:00 (UTC) on July 4, 2025, involving OP, CRV, CAKE, ENS, RUNE, ROSE, ORDI, SUSHI, ONE, ALT, MANTA and other currencies. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Binance to Update Collateral Ratios for Portfolio Margin Assets
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to update the collateral ratios for several assets under its Portfolio Margin service. This update is scheduled to take place on 2025-07-04 at 06:00 (UTC) and is expected to be completed within approximately 30 minutes.

The assets affected by this update include OP, CRV, CAKE, ENS, RUNE, ROSE, ORDI, SUSHI, ONE, ALT, and MANTA. The collateral ratios for these assets will be adjusted as follows: OP and CRV will see their ratios decrease from 75% to 65%, CAKE and ENS from 70% to 60%, RUNE from 70% to 55%, ROSE from 55% to 40%, ORDI and SUSHI from 60% to 40%, ONE from 55% to 40%, ALT from 50% to 35%, and MANTA from 50% to 30%.

Users are advised to closely monitor their Unified Maintenance Margin Ratio (uniMMR) as the changes in collateral ratios could impact it. This adjustment may lead to potential liquidation or losses if not managed properly. Binance emphasizes the importance of staying informed about these changes to mitigate any risks associated with the updated collateral ratios.

data.title: Trump Considers Extending Tariff Suspension Amid Trade Talks with Canada
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. President Donald Trump stated in an interview that he believes there is no need to extend the tariff suspension period, although an extension is possible. Trump also announced the suspension of trade negotiations with Canada until they cease certain tax practices.

data.title: Stablecoin Adoption Surpasses Traditional Card Networks Globally
data.description:
Alchemy reports stablecoin adoption now exceeds traditional card networks, impacting global payments and DeFi ecosystems.
source: https://coincu.com/345866-stablecoin-adoption-surpasses-traditional-networks

data.title: Stablecoins Surpass Traditional Card Networks in Adoption, Says Alchemy Executive
data.description: According to Odaily, Alchemy's engineering director Noam Hurwitz has highlighted the growing significance of stablecoins in internet payments. He noted that stablecoins have now surpassed major traditional card networks in adoption, experiencing explosive growth and becoming the default settlement layer for online transactions. Companies like PayPal and Stripe are integrating stablecoins to leverage blockchain infrastructure, enabling faster and cheaper transactions. The attributes of stablecoins, such as being inexpensive, fast, global, and secure for transfers, have made them popular for various applications, including cross-border payments and prediction markets like Polymarket.

data.title: Stablecoins are becoming ‘default settlement layer’ for internet: Alchemy
data.description:
Stablecoins are now the “default settlement layer for the internet,” surpassing Visa and Mastercard in onchain transaction volume.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/stablecoins-default-settlement-layer-internet-alchemy?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Alchemy: Stablecoins are becoming the “default settlement layer” of the internet
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Noam Hurwitz, head of engineering at Alchemy, said that stablecoins have become the mainstay of Internet payments, and their adoption rate has now exceeded that of major traditional card networks. The adoption rate of stablecoins has "exploded" and is becoming the default settlement layer of the Internet. Companies such as PayPal and Stripe are integrating stablecoins to leverage on-chain infrastructure for faster and cheaper transactions. Stablecoins make currency "cheap, fast, global and secure to transfer." These features make them popular for a variety of purposes and are widely adopted in cross-border payments and prediction markets such as Polymarket. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bonk Bounces Back – But Is It Just a Flicker or the Start of a Fire?
data.description:
"Bonk has made a remarkable comeback, showing signs of resilience and recovery in its recent performance. However, one must consider whether this resurgence is merely a temporary spark—an ephemeral flicker of enthusiasm—or if it signifies the beginning of a lasting and impactful fire that could lead to sustained success and growth in the future."
source: https://blocknews.com/bonk-bounces-back-but-is-it-just-a-flicker-or-the-start-of-a-fire/
